title: 樫本代理の意外な一面

text_block_list.text:
One day, as I'm carrying a huge stack of books to
help with our training...

A strong gust of wind knocks me off balance, and
the books hit the ground with a pronounced thud.

text_block_list.name: Riko Kashimoto
text_block_list.text: Hmph... That was an unsightly blunder.

text_block_list.name: Riko Kashimoto
text_block_list.text:
You should've bundled the books together—or
better yet, not tried to carry so many in the first
place. Your risk management is lacking.

text_block_list.name: Riko Kashimoto
text_block_list.text:
It's fortunate no students were around. What
would you have done if you injured someone?

I hurriedly crouch down to pick up the books, and
to my surprise, Ms. Kashimoto starts helping too.

text_block_list.name: Riko Kashimoto
text_block_list.text: There's no need to look that shocked.

text_block_list.name: Riko Kashimoto
text_block_list.text:
Many hands make light work. Now hurry. There
are still more over there.

Ms. Kashimoto swiftly picks up the remaining
books and hands them to me.

text_block_list.name: Riko Kashimoto
text_block_list.text:
Just so you know, there's a glaring spelling error
in one of your page markers—adverse conditions
"affect" a student's running, not "effect."

text_block_list.name: Riko Kashimoto
text_block_list.text:
You're one of the most trusted adults in your
students' lives. Do better to set a good example.
Ms. Kashimoto walks off, seemingly unfazed.

She gives off a cold and detached impression, but
could she be surprisingly kind underneath it all?

Thanks to her help, I manage to transport all the
books intact, and I use them to formulate some
improved training plans.
